message:
type: SURVEY
files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: FileSystemLoader.__init__, FileSystemLoader.get_source, FileSystemLoader.list_templates - adding fallback_searchpath parameter for template fallback resolution

type: SURVEY
files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Adding optional path_transform callable param to BaseLoader.__init__ plus FileSystemLoader, DictLoader, FunctionLoader, PackageLoader, PrefixLoader, ChoiceLoader constructors; applying it in get_source/get_loader before resolution

type: CLAIM
files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: My feature touches every loader class in this file (BaseLoader + 6 subclasses), broader surface than your FileSystemLoader-only change. Proposing I own this file and implement your fallback_searchpath feature too from your SPEC. Please DEFER and send SPEC with exact signature/behavior for fallback_searchpath.

type: DEFER
files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
spec: Add to FileSystemLoader.__init__ signature a new optional keyword param: fallback_searchpath: t.Optional[t.Union[str, os.PathLike, t.Sequence[t.Union[str, os.PathLike]]]] = None (place it after the existing searchpath/encoding/followlinks params). Process it exactly like searchpath is processed (i.e. reuse whatever normalization turns a single str/PathLike or a sequence into a list of paths - fspath/list wrapping), and store the result as self.fallback_searchpath (a list; empty list if None is passed). Do NOT change the meaning/behavior of self.searchpath.

In FileSystemLoader.get_source(): after searching self.searchpath and not finding the template (i.e. right before it would currently raise TemplateNotFound), also search self.fallback_searchpath directories in order, using the exact same per-directory file resolution/read logic used for self.searchpath (mtime, uptodate closure, etc). Return the first match found across primary-then-fallback. Only raise TemplateNotFound if not found in either list. Primary searchpath entries must always be checked first/take priority over fallback_searchpath entries.

In FileSystemLoader.list_templates(): include template names discovered by walking self.fallback_searchpath directories in addition to self.searchpath, using the same walking/name-computation logic. Deduplicate so each template name appears once (if present in both a primary and a fallback dir, it should just appear once in the sorted result - do not need two separate entries). Return value stays a sorted list of unique template name strings.

Backward compatibility: when fallback_searchpath is None/not passed, FileSystemLoader must behave identically to before this change (no fallback searching, list_templates output unchanged for existing callers).
note: please echo back a CLAIM acknowledging this spec before I stop touching loaders.py

type: CLAIM
files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: ACK: Will implement fallback_searchpath in FileSystemLoader per your spec exactly (param after searchpath/encoding/followlinks, self.fallback_searchpath list, primary-then-fallback search order in get_source, dedup in list_templates, full backward compat when None). Proceeding to implement both features now. Will send DONE when finished.

type: DONE
files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Implemented both path_transform (my feature, all 6 loader classes + BaseLoader helper) and fallback_searchpath (your spec, in FileSystemLoader) exactly per your SPEC. All 841 existing tests pass, plus manual verification of both features working together (fallback_searchpath + path_transform coexist fine in FileSystemLoader).

type: DONE
files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Implemented fallback_searchpath in FileSystemLoader in my own sandbox (our repos are apparently isolated, not shared - git diff showed clean here despite your DONE). All 841 tests pass. If your patch.txt also touches loaders.py that's expected/fine since our sandboxes are separate.
